name: Fontanarejo from Horcajo de los Montes — circular by the río Valdehornos and Alcoba de Montes
name_original: Fontanarejo desde Horcajo de los Montes — circular por el río Valdehornos y Alcoba de Montes
region_intro: Road cycling around Horcajo De Los Montes offers varied gradients within the Montes de Toledo mountain range. The region features hilly terrain and well-maintained roads, providing diverse options for cyclists. Its location within or bordering the Cabañeros National Park contributes to its natural appeal. Cyclists can expect quiet roads and panoramic views across the landscape. Best road cycling routes around Horcajo De Los Montes The most popular road cycling route is Fontanarejo from Horcajo de los Montes — circular by the río Valdehornos and Alcoba de Montes, a 35.7 miles (57.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 45 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores the area around the Valdehornos river. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Cabañeros National Park and Torre de Abraham Reservoir Loop, a difficult 75.5 miles (121.5 km) path. This route traverses the Cabañeros National Park and circles the Torre de Abraham Reservoir. Local road cyclists also love the From Horcajo de los Montes through the Cabañeros National Park — circular route around the Torre de Abraham Reservoir, a 75.6 miles (121.6 km) trail leading through Cabañeros National Park, often completed in about 5 hours 3 minutes. Road cycling around Horcajo De Los Montes is defined by hilly terrain, the Cabañeros National Park, and the Montes de Toledo mountain range. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Horcajo de los Montes?
A: There are 7 road cycling routes available around Horcajo de los Montes. These routes offer varied gradients within the Montes de Toledo mountain range, catering to different fitness levels.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or less experienced cyclists?
A: While there are no routes specifically rated as 'easy', you can find 2 moderate routes around Horcajo de los Montes. The route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1179507019/fontanarejo-from-horcajo-de-los-montes-circular-by-the-rio-valdehornos-and-alcoba-de-montes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontanarejo from Horcajo de los Montes — circular by the río Valdehornos and Alcoba de Montes&lt;/a&gt; is a good option, covering 57.4 km with a moderate difficulty.

Q: What kind of challenge can I expect from road cycling routes in this region?
A: The region is characterized by hilly terrain, offering varied gradients. Most routes are rated as difficult, with 5 out of 7 routes falling into this category. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025236984/montes-de-toledo-and-cabaneros-park-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montes de Toledo and Cabañeros Park Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 116.7 km ride with significant elevation gain.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, several routes offer long-distance cycling. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067730682/cabaneros-national-park-and-torre-de-abraham-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañeros National Park and Torre de Abraham Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 121.5 km path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1366179733/from-horcajo-de-los-montes-through-the-cabaneros-national-park-circular-route-around-the-torre-de-abraham-reservoir" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Horcajo de los Montes through the Cabañeros National Park — circular route around the Torre de Abraham Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; is a similar 121.6 km trail.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes around Horcajo de los Montes?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in the area are circular. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1179507019/fontanarejo-from-horcajo-de-los-montes-circular-by-the-rio-valdehornos-and-alcoba-de-montes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontanarejo from Horcajo de los Montes — circular by the río Valdehornos and Alcoba de Montes&lt;/a&gt;, and the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1067730682/cabaneros-national-park-and-torre-de-abraham-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabañeros National Park and Torre de Abraham Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of landscapes will I see while road cycling here?
A: You will experience diverse and captivating landscapes, including hilly terrain, extensive pastures, and mountains within the Cabañeros National Park. Routes often feature multiple viewpoints offering spectacular panoramas of the Montes de Toledo and the national park's pristine Mediterranean forest ecosystems.

Q: Are there any interesting natural landmarks or attractions along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several natural attractions. You might encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4413243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estena Towers&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2839746" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Rocigalgo&lt;/a&gt; summit, or even the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5633420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera Chica Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The Cabañeros National Park itself is a major draw, known for its rich biodiversity.

Q: Is it possible to spot wildlife while road cycling in Horcajo de los Montes?
A: Cycling through the Cabañeros National Park offers a chance to experience pristine Mediterranean forest ecosystems, which shelter a wide variety of bird and mammal species. Keep an eye out for large birds of prey, black storks, eagles, and vultures, especially in the quieter sections of the park.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Horcajo de los Montes?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 2.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the quiet, well-maintained roads and the breathtaking panoramic views, particularly those found within the Cabañeros National Park.

Q: Are the roads generally quiet and safe for road cycling?
A: Yes, the roads in the vicinity of Horcajo de los Montes are generally in good condition with little traffic, making for a safer and more enjoyable cycling experience. This allows riders to fully immerse themselves in the natural beauty of the region.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Horcajo de los Montes?
A: The region's Mediterranean climate makes spring and autumn ideal for road cycling, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler temperatures, especially at higher elevations.
tips: Horcajo has a unique charm: its municipal term is integrated into the Cabañeros National Park. 1Km from the urban center of Horcajo, is the largest visitor center in the Park, essential to plan a visit. There, a main exhibition shows the different ecosystems of Cabañeros throughout the four seasons of the year. In addition, a farmhouse in which the culture and traditions of the region are disclosed. It is completed with projection rooms, a library, a picnic area, car parks, etc.

The Plaza de la Iglesia in Alcoba de los Montes is the heart of the town and a historical and cultural landmark.

It is located next to the Church of Our Lady of Consolation, right in the town center.

Fontanarejo is a historic town founded in the 15th century that is part of the Montes de Toledo region near the Sierra de Valdeja, at the foot of the southern mountain of the Arroba-Navalpino anticline depression.
The bridge over the Valdehornos river on the CM-4103 road. There is an archaeological site of an ancient settlement on the banks of the river.
